title: Weekly Insights Review
summary:
Read here everything you need to know


Latest Trends on fuel transition, shipyard actions, government, ports, financing and industry players
Read More

India's already low coal inventories have taken a beating as the temperatures have been building
Temperatures in India have been soaring to unprecedented levels in recent days and weeks, putting lives, crops and energy supplies at risk. Read More

Snapshot of Spot Freight Rates, Supply-Demand Trends, Port Congestions
The freight market sentiment sustained the firmness of the last days with revival seen in the Capesize and Handysize segment, and Panamax recorded a gradual upward trend. Read More

Temperatures turning even hotter in India is becoming another positive development
Coal India produced approximately 53.5 million tons of coal last month, which marks a month-on-month decline of 26.6 million tons (-33%) but a year-on-year increase of 11.6 million tons (28%).

children.title: Energy managed to buck the trend with supply side issues pushing prices higher
section_path: Energy managed to buck the trend with supply side issues pushing prices higher
children.section_path_text: Energy managed to buck the trend with supply side issues pushing prices higher
children.summary: Mounting growth concerns weigh on commodity markets, with metals leading the complex lower. Energy managed to buck the trend with supply side issues pushing prices higher.

children.title: The first half was not promising for the performance of freight rates as the dry bulk demand was also in a downward trend.
section_path: The first half was not promising for the performance of freight rates as the dry bulk demand was also in a downward trend.
children.section_path_text: The first half was not promising for the performance of freight rates as the dry bulk demand was also in a downward trend.
children.summary:
The current year presents year to date an overall slower dry bulk demand growth in ton days with an aggressive downward correction seen from March onwards. Read More
